assertions.reason: On Ethereum compare, a router quote rendered while the submit button still showed 'Comparing...', confirming first-result progressive rendering before stream completion.
With one quote already visible, the pending router tab displayed 'Loading...', confirming a user-visible pending indicator.
After both quotes arrived, the UI showed both router tabs plus a populated reason box and recommendation state.

During auto-refresh, countdown advanced and refreshed results, but no user-visible progressive in-flight state ('Comparing...' / tab 'Loading...') was observable; UI appeared to jump directly between settled states.
Could not verify whether countdown reset waits for both router responses because auto-refresh in-flight progressive state was not user-visible in this environment (see VAL-PROG-010 failure).
On Base (single-router expectation), UI showed dual loading tabs and then 'Failed to connect to quote stream' instead of rendering a single successful quote flow without unnecessary Curve loading.
evidence: curl compare-stream chainId=8453 returned curve error + spandex quote + complete

frictions: agent-browser session intermittently reset to about:blank during longer observation loops; reopened the same named session and repeated affected checks.
agent-browser network/console capture was unreliable for EventSource details, so curl was used for supporting stream evidence.
blockers: VAL-PROG-011 remained blocked because auto-refresh did not expose observable progressive pending state needed to verify countdown reset timing against per-router completion.
